fir.intro: An FIR is the first official complaint recorded by police for a cognizable offence.
steps: Go to the nearest police station.
Explain the incident clearly with date, time, and place.
Submit written complaint or give oral statement.
Provide proof or evidence if available.
Police records the FIR and gives acknowledgement or FIR copy.
Investigation starts after registration.
documents: Photos, videos, or screenshots if available
important_notes: Check whether all facts are correctly written before signing.
If police refuse, complaint may be escalated to higher authority.
fir.next_step: Visit the nearest police station and keep your supporting evidence ready.

divorce.topic: How to File a Divorce Case
divorce.intro: Divorce is the legal process of ending a marriage through family court.
steps: Consult a lawyer and identify whether it is mutual or contested divorce.
Prepare petition with marriage details and grounds for divorce.
File the petition in the appropriate family court.
Court sends notice to the opposite party.
Both sides appear before court.
Mediation or counselling may happen.
If unresolved, the court hears the matter and passes decree.
documents: Supporting proof for grounds of divorce
Photos or communication records if relevant
important_notes: Child custody and maintenance issues may also be decided.
Mutual divorce is usually simpler than contested divorce.
divorce.next_step: Consult a family lawyer and prepare required documents for filing.

cyber complaint.topic: How to File a Cyber Crime Complaint
cyber complaint.intro: Cyber complaints can be filed for online fraud, hacking, fake accounts, blackmail, or digital abuse.
steps: Preserve screenshots, transaction proof, and account details.
Do not delete chats, emails, or messages.
File complaint at cyber crime police station or online cyber portal.
Provide all digital evidence clearly.
Police or cyber cell reviews the complaint and may start investigation.
important_notes: Report quickly to improve recovery chances in fraud cases.
cyber complaint.next_step: Collect all digital proof and file complaint with cyber crime authorities immediately.

domestic violence.topic: How to File a Domestic Violence Complaint
domestic violence.intro: Domestic violence complaints may be filed for physical, emotional, verbal, sexual, or economic abuse.
steps: Ensure immediate safety first.
Approach police, protection officer, or magistrate.
Explain abuse details clearly.
Submit medical proof, photos, messages, or witness details if available.
Seek protection order, residence order, or maintenance if needed.
important_notes: Medical documentation should be done as early as possible.
Legal protection and shelter support may also be available.
domestic violence.next_step: Prioritize safety and contact police, legal aid, or protection officer.
